subject: An aerial view of a vast cornfield scored with overlapping concentric crop-circle patterns, with scattered farm buildings and bright cyan-blue energy fractures or portals torn open across the field
description: A high-angle, almost top-down view of a pale-green and tan cornfield stretching to the horizon. The field is etched with multiple overlapping concentric ring patterns — crop-circle iconography — flattened into the standing crop, varying in size and arranged in a non-random cluster across the lower two-thirds of the image. Scattered across the field are several glowing cyan-blue rectangular fractures or rifts, reading as portals, broken windows lying flat, or wedges of fractured sky embedded in the soil. Two darker structures sit in the upper-left and upper-mid — a slim tower or silo and what appears to be a small farm building. A line of green hedge or tree growth crosses the lower foreground edge. No figures are depicted. Palette is muted: taupe, dusty sage, soft cream, with the cold blue energy serving as the only chromatic accent.
foreground: dark green hedgerow or tree growth along the bottom edge of the field
background: aerial cornfield with concentric crop circles, scattered farm buildings, cyan-blue portal fractures
